calendar.matters: PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE CALENDAR FOR ATTORNEY CHRISTOPHER WURSTER

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY CHRISTOPHER WURSTER
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Continued To Sep-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Apperances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. STEPHANIE L. GONZALEZ, Esq. of Donahue Fitzgerald LLP (tel: 510-451-3300) for Petitioner.
Atty. CHRISTOPHER WURSTER, Esq. of Burns & Levinson LLP (tel: 617-345-3000), Pro Hac Vice application pending.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER KRISTIN KNOUSE , M.D., PH.D.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Matter discussed. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court grants counsel Christopher Wurster's pro hac vice application.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Petition To Enforce Subpoena Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2029.600
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Petition To Enforce Subpoena
Off Calendar May-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KRISTIN KNOUSE Notice Of Motion And Petition To Enforce Subpoena

Motion is denied for failure to comply with C.C.P. 1005, 1010.6, and related service/notice requirements. The proof of service accompanying the moving papers shows that moving papers were served electronically on May 6, 2024 upon the other party with the hearing set for May 24, 2024 which provided only 13 court days' of notice. The Court therefore has no choice but to deny the motion.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-22-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER KRISTIN KNOUSE's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. The motion was untimely filed and served per CCP 1005.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
